Human.name: Dual Talent
Human.description: Some humans are uniquely skilled at maximizing their natural talents. These humans pick two ability scores and gain a +2 racial bonus in each. This replaces the bonus feat and skilled traits.

Human.name: Heart of the Fields
Human.description: Humans with strong ties to the land can ignore one Fatigue effect 1/day and gain +half their character level to one Craft or Profession skill.
Human.grants: Ignore 1 Fatigue 1/day; +halfLevel to one Craft or Profession

Human.name: Heart of the Streets
Human.description: Urban humans gain +1 to Reflex saves and +1 to CMD against bull rush and trip attempts when in crowded areas.
Human.grants: +1 CMD vs bull rush/trip in crowds

Human.name: Heart of the Wilderness
Human.description: Wilderness-adapted humans gain +half their character level to Survival checks and gain +5 to stabilize when dying.

Human.name: Eye for Talent
Human.description: These humans are adept at recognizing potential in animal companions and familiars. The human's animal companion or familiar gains a +2 bonus to one ability score of the human's choice.
Human.grants: +2 to animal companion or familiar's chosen ability

Human.name: Focused Study
Human.description: Rather than gaining a bonus feat at 1st level, a human with this trait gains the Skill Focus feat in a skill of their choice. At 8th and 16th level, they can retrain their Skill Focus to a different skill.

Human.name: Heroic
Human.description: Some humans are born heroes, blessed with the gift of inspiration. These humans gain 1 Hero Point at character creation and gain an additional Hero Point at each level.

Human.name: Silver Tongued
Human.description: Humans with this trait gain +2 to Diplomacy and Bluff checks. They can shift a creature's attitude by up to 3 steps with a successful Diplomacy check (instead of the normal 1 step).
Human.grants: Can shift attitude up to 3 steps with Diplomacy

Human.name: Adoptive Parentage
Human.description: Humans adopted and raised by other races gain one racial trait from the foster parent's race as long as the human meets the prerequisites.
Human.grants: One racial trait from another race

Human.name: Awareness
Human.description: Humans with this trait gain +1 to Perception and Sense Motive checks. Both Perception and Sense Motive are always class skills for them.
Human.grants: Perception and Sense Motive are always class skills

Dwarf.name: Deep Delver
Dwarf.description: Dwarves with this trait gain +2 to Dungeoneering checks and can automatically determine how deep underground they are within 100 feet.

Dwarf.name: Giant Hunter
Dwarf.description: These dwarves are specially trained in combat against giants. They gain +1 to attack rolls against creatures of Large or larger size and +2 dodge AC against such creatures.
Dwarf.grants: +2 dodge AC vs Large or larger creatures

Dwarf.name: Lorekeeper
Dwarf.description: Dwarven lorekeepers are repositories of ancient knowledge. They gain +2 to Knowledge (History) checks and can make Knowledge (History) checks untrained.

Dwarf.name: Craftsman
Dwarf.description: These dwarves are exceptional craftspeople. They gain +2 to Craft or Profession checks related to creating metal or stone objects.

Dwarf.name: Rock Stepper
Dwarf.description: Dwarves with this trait can ignore difficult terrain caused by rubble, loose stone, or uneven stone surfaces.
Dwarf.grants: Ignore rubble and stone difficult terrain

Dwarf.name: Stubborn
Dwarf.description: Dwarves with this trait gain +2 to Will saves against charm and compulsion effects. Additionally, they can reroll a failed saving throw against such effects once per day.

Dwarf.name: Wyrmscourged
Dwarf.description: These dwarves are experienced dragon slayers. They gain +1 to attack rolls against dragons and +2 to Perception checks to notice dragons and dragon-related traps.

Dwarf.name: Surface Survivalist
Dwarf.description: These dwarves have adapted to surface life. They have low-light vision instead of darkvision and gain +2 to Survival checks when on the surface.

Elf.name: Arctic Elf
Elf.description: Elves adapted to arctic environments gain +2 to Perception checks in snow and ice conditions and gain cold resistance 5.

Elf.name: Desert Runner
Elf.description: Desert elves gain +4 to Constitution checks to resist the effects of a hot environment and suffer fatigue one step less (fatigued instead of exhausted, or nothing instead of fatigued).

Elf.name: Woodlands Elf
Elf.description: Elves of the deep forests gain +4 to Stealth checks when in a forest environment.

Elf.name: Dreamspeaker
Elf.description: These elves gain +1 to the DC of divination spells they cast. At 15th level, they can cast dream once per day.
Elf.grants: +1 DC divination spells; dream 1/day at 15th level

Elf.name: Envoy
Elf.description: Diplomatic elves gain +1 to Bluff, Diplomacy, and Knowledge (Nobility) checks. They can use spells with the charm descriptor to affect animals as though they were humanoids.

Elf.name: Fleet-Footed
Elf.description: These elves gain the Run feat as a bonus feat. Additionally, they gain +2 to initiative checks.

Elf.name: Lightbringer
Elf.description: Elves devoted to light magic gain +1 to the caster level of their light-based spells and gain +1 to turn undead checks.
Elf.grants: +1 CL for light spells; +1 turn undead

Elf.name: Silent Hunter
Elf.description: Forest-dwelling elves gain +2 to Perception checks in forest environments and reduce the Stealth penalty for moving at normal speed by 5.
Elf.grants: Stealth penalty for movement reduced by 5

Gnome.name: Pyromaniac
Gnome.description: These gnomes are obsessed with fire magic. They gain +1 to the caster level of fire spells and can cast dancing lights, flare, prestidigitation, and produce flame as spell-like abilities.
Gnome.grants: +1 CL fire spells; dancing lights, flare, prestidigitation, produce flame 1/day each

Gnome.name: Fell Magic
Gnome.description: Dark-minded gnomes gain +1 to the DC of necromancy spells they cast. They can cast bleed, chill touch, detect poison, and touch of fatigue as spell-like abilities.
Gnome.grants: +1 DC necromancy spells; bleed, chill touch, detect poison, touch of fatigue 1/day each

Gnome.name: Eternal Hope
Gnome.description: Optimistic gnomes gain +2 to saving throws against fear effects. Additionally, once per day they can reroll a natural 1 on any d20 roll they just made.

Gnome.name: Nosophobia
Gnome.description: Disease-wary gnomes gain +4 to saving throws against disease. They can reroll a failed saving throw against a disease once per day.

Gnome.name: Gift of Tongues
Gnome.description: Linguistically gifted gnomes gain +1 to Bluff and Diplomacy checks. They can learn a new language after spending 1 week of continuous interaction with a new culture.

Gnome.name: Master Tinker
Gnome.description: Masterful gnome crafters gain +1 to Disable Device and Knowledge (Engineering) checks. They are proficient with any weapon they craft themselves.

Halfling.name: Fleet of Foot
Halfling.description: These halflings are remarkably swift. Their base speed increases to 30 feet instead of the normal 20 feet.

Halfling.name: Warslinger
Halfling.description: Halfling slingers can reload their slings as a free action instead of a move action.

Halfling.name: Underfoot
Halfling.description: These halflings are experts at staying out of larger foes' way. They gain +1 dodge AC against Large or larger creatures and +1 to Acrobatics checks to move through threatened squares.
Halfling.grants: +1 dodge AC vs Large or larger foes

Halfling.name: Outrider
Halfling.description: Mounted halflings gain +2 to Handle Animal and Ride checks.

Halfling.name: Adaptable Luck
Halfling.description: These halflings' luck is more flexible. Three times per day, they can add +2 luck bonus to any ability check, skill check, or saving throw they just made.

Halfling.name: Polyglot
Halfling.description: Linguistically skilled halflings gain +2 to Linguistics checks. They start with one bonus language per point of Intelligence modifier.
Halfling.grants: One bonus language per INT modifier

Half-Elf.name: Ancestral Arms
Half-Elf.description: Half-elves with strong ties to their heritage gain proficiency with one martial or exotic weapon of their choice.
Half-Elf.grants: Proficiency with one martial or exotic weapon

Half-Elf.name: Dual Minded
Half-Elf.description: These half-elves' mixed heritage grants them exceptional willpower. They gain +2 to Will saves.

Half-Elf.name: Water Child
Half-Elf.description: Half-elves raised near water gain +4 to Swim checks and can hold their breath for additional rounds equal to their Constitution modifier.
Half-Elf.grants: Hold breath extra rounds equal to CON modifier

Half-Elf.name: Integrated
Half-Elf.description: Half-elves who move between cultures gain +1 to Bluff, Disguise, and Knowledge (Local) checks.

Half-Elf.name: Sociable
Half-Elf.description: Charismatic half-elves gain a unique ability: when a Diplomacy check fails, they can attempt the check again as an immediate action with a -5 penalty.
Half-Elf.grants: Retry failed Diplomacy as immediate action at -5

Half-Orc.name: Shaman's Apprentice
Half-Orc.description: Half-orcs trained in shamanic traditions gain the Endurance feat as a bonus feat.

Half-Orc.name: Toothy
Half-Orc.description: These half-orcs have prominent tusks that serve as a weapon. They gain a bite attack that deals 1d4 damage.

Half-Orc.name: Sacred Tattoo
Half-Orc.description: Half-orcs marked with sacred tattoos gain +1 luck bonus to all saving throws.

Half-Orc.name: Skilled
Half-Orc.description: Some half-orcs gain an additional skill rank per level (like humans), instead of having darkvision.

Half-Orc.name: Cavewight
Half-Orc.description: Underground-dwelling half-orcs gain +1 to Knowledge (Dungeoneering) and Survival checks made underground.

Half-Orc.name: Scavenger
Half-Orc.description: Half-orcs with keen senses gain +2 to Appraise and Perception checks made to find hidden objects and treasures.

Catfolk.name: Climber
Catfolk.description: These catfolk are natural climbers. They gain a 20-foot climb speed instead of the increased land speed.

Catfolk.name: Curiosity
Catfolk.description: Inquisitive catfolk gain +4 to Diplomacy checks to gather information and +1 to Knowledge (History) and Knowledge (Local) checks.

Catfolk.name: Nimble Faller
Catfolk.description: These catfolk always land on their feet. They take no falling damage from falls of 20 feet or less and treat all falls as if 20 feet shorter than actual.
Catfolk.grants: Reduce falling damage 20 ft; always land on feet

Kitsune.name: Fast Shifter
Kitsune.description: These kitsune can change their shape as a move action instead of a standard action, allowing them to shift and act in the same round.

Kitsune.name: Keen Kitsune
Kitsune.description: Intellectually gifted kitsune gain +2 to Intelligence instead of +2 to Dexterity.

Kitsune.name: Gregarious
Kitsune.description: Socially adept kitsune gain +2 to Diplomacy checks. When they succeed at a Diplomacy check, the target becomes friendly for 24 hours instead of the normal duration.
Kitsune.grants: Make target friendly for 24 hours with successful Diplomacy

Tengu.name: Glide
Tengu.description: These tengu can glide on air currents. They take no falling damage from any distance and can glide, moving 5 feet forward for every 20 feet they fall.
Tengu.grants: No fall damage; glide 5 ft per 20 ft fallen

Tengu.name: Claw Attack
Tengu.description: Tengu with this trait have prominent claws. They gain two claw attacks that deal 1d3 damage each. They do not lose proficiency with simple weapons.

Tengu.name: Carrion Sense
Tengu.description: These tengu have exceptional senses for death. They gain scent ability, but only for corpses and dying creatures within 15 feet.
Tengu.grants: Scent for corpses and dying creatures within 15 ft

Goblin.name: Tree Runner
Goblin.description: Forest-dwelling goblins are excellent climbers. They gain a 30-foot climb speed.

Goblin.name: Hard Head Big Teeth
Goblin.description: These goblins have harder skulls and larger teeth. They gain a bite attack that deals 1d4 damage.

Goblin.name: Oversized Ears
Goblin.description: Goblins with outsized ears gain +4 to Perception checks.

Orc.name: Dayrunner
Orc.description: These orcs have adapted to surface life and no longer suffer from light sensitivity. However, they take -2 to ranged attack rolls in bright light.

Orc.name: Smeller
Orc.description: Orcs with keen senses gain the scent ability, allowing them to detect creatures and objects by smell within 30 feet.

Orc.name: Squalid
Orc.description: Orcs inured to filth gain +2 to Fortitude saving throws against disease and nauseating effects.

Ifrit.name: Wildfire Heart
Ifrit.description: These fire genasi are unpredictably volatile. They gain +4 to initiative checks.

Ifrit.name: Fire in the Blood
Ifrit.description: When an ifrit takes fire damage, they gain fast healing 2 for 1 round.
Ifrit.grants: Fast healing 2 for 1 round when taking fire damage

Oread.name: Crystalline Form
Oread.description: These earth genasi have crystalline structures within their bodies. They gain +2 AC against rays and a 10% miss chance from ranged attacks.

Oread.name: Treacherous Earth
Oread.description: Once per day, an oread with this trait can create difficult terrain in a 10-foot radius for 1 round as a standard action.

Sylph.name: Whispering Wind
Sylph.description: These air genasi are experts at moving silently. They gain +4 to Stealth checks.

Sylph.name: Breeze-Kissed
Sylph.description: Sylphs blessed by gentle winds gain +2 AC against non-magical ranged attacks.

Undine.name: Amphibious
Undine.description: These water genasi can breathe water as easily as air, allowing them to exist in aquatic environments indefinitely.

Undine.name: Water Sense
Undine.description: Undines with this trait have extraordinary sensory awareness underwater. They gain blindsense 30 feet while in water.
